en: "Call now; is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
zh: “你只管呼籲吧，有誰回答你？ 在眾聖者之中你轉向哪一位？

en: Surely vexation kills the fool, and jealousy slays the simple.
zh: 煩惱殺死愚昧人， 激情害死愚蒙人。

en: I have seen the fool taking root, but suddenly I cursed his dwelling.
zh: 我看見愚昧人扎下了根， 但咒詛忽然臨到他的居所。

en: His children are far from safety; they are crushed in the gate, and there is no one to deliver them.
zh: 他的兒女遠離安穩之處， 在城門口被欺壓卻沒有人援救。

en: The hungry eat his harvest, and he takes it even out of thorns, and the thirsty pant after his wealth.
zh: 飢餓的人吃盡他的莊稼， 連在荊棘裡的也搶去； 口渴的人 吞盡他的財富。

en: For affliction does not come from the dust, nor does trouble sprout from the ground,
zh: 患難不從土中生出來， 勞碌不由地裡長出來；

en: but man is born to trouble as the sparks fly upward.
zh: 原來人為勞碌而生， 如同火花向上飛揚。

en: "As for me, I would seek God, and to God would I commit my cause,
zh: 至於我，我必尋求 神， 向他陳明我的案件。

en: who does great things and unsearchable, marvelous things without number:
zh: 他所作的大事無法測度， 所行的奇事不可勝數。

en: he gives rain on the earth and sends waters on the fields;
zh: 降雨在地上， 遣水到田裡，

en: he sets on high those who are lowly, and those who mourn are lifted to safety.
zh: 把低微的安置在高位， 把哀慟的高舉於安穩之處；

en: He frustrates the devices of the crafty, so that their hands achieve no success.
zh: 又挫敗狡猾人的計謀， 使他們的手所作的無法成全；

en: He catches the wise in their own craftiness, and the schemes of the wily are brought to a quick end.
zh: 他使有智慧的人中了自己的詭計， 使奸詐人的謀算快快失敗。

en: They meet with darkness in the daytime and grope at noonday as in the night.
zh: 他們白天遇見黑暗， 中午摸索如在夜間。

en: But he saves the needy from the sword of their mouth and from the hand of the mighty.
zh: 他拯救窮乏人脫離他們口中的刀劍， 脫離強暴人的手；

en: So the poor have hope, and injustice shuts her mouth.
zh: 這樣，貧窮人有指望， 不義的人卻閉口無言。

en: "Behold, blessed is the one whom God reproves; therefore despise not the discipline of the Almighty.
zh: 神所責備的人是有福的， 所以你不要輕看全能者的管教。

en: For he wounds, but he binds up; he shatters, but his hands heal.
zh: 因為他打傷，又纏裹； 他擊傷，又親手醫治。

en: He will deliver you from six troubles; in seven no evil shall touch you.
zh: 你六次遭遇患難，他都救拔你， 就算第七次，災禍也傷不了你。

en: In famine he will redeem you from death, and in war from the power of the sword.
zh: 在饑荒中，他必救你脫離死亡； 在戰爭中，他必救你脫離刀劍的權勢。

en: You shall be hidden from the lash of the tongue, and shall not fear destruction when it comes.
zh: 你必不受人口舌之害， 災殃臨到，也不害怕。

en: At destruction and famine you shall laugh, and shall not fear the beasts of the earth.
zh: 對災殃和饑饉，一笑置之， 地上的野獸你也不害怕。

en: For you shall be in league with the stones of the field, and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with you.
zh: 你必與田間的石頭立約， 田間的野獸必與你和平相處。

en: You shall know that your tent is at peace, and you shall inspect your fold and miss nothing.
zh: 你必曉得你的帳棚平安無事； 你查看莊舍，也一無缺失。

en: You shall know also that your offspring shall be many, and your descendants as the grass of the earth.
zh: 你必曉得你的後裔眾多， 子孫像地上的青草那樣茂盛。

en: You shall come to your grave in ripe old age, like a sheaf gathered up in its season.
zh: 你必全壽才歸到墳墓去， 如同禾捆到時就收起來。

en: Behold, this we have searched out; it is true. Hear, and know it for your good."
zh: 這道理我們已經查明實在是這樣， 你當聆聽，也當明白。”
